breaking_news.title: LeakBase Cybercrime Forum Seized in Global Takedown
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: The FBI and Europol-led Operation Leak dismantled LeakBase, one of the world’s largest cybercrime forums used to trade stolen credentials, stealer logs, and hacking tools. Active since 2021, the platform hosted over 142,000 registered users and more than 215,000 messages as of December 2025. Law enforcement seized its domains, disrupted infrastructure in 14 countries, and executed search warrants and arrests across the U.S., UK, Spain, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Belgium, and Australia.

Paragraph 2: The takedown disrupts a central hub of the cybercrime ecosystem, significantly impeding credential stuffing, ransomware affiliate recruitment, and exploit trading. This operation is one of the largest coordinated actions against a data marketplace in recent years and signals a major escalation in public-private cooperation to dismantle illicit underground markets.

breaking_news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishing Platform Dismantled, Disrupting MFA Bypass at Scale
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Microsoft-led international operation dismantled Tycoon 2FA, a major phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform used to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) via adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) attacks. Europol coordinated the takedown across 14 countries, seizing 330 domains and infrastructure used to generate fraudulent login pages and credential harvesting proxies. By mid-2025, Tycoon 2FA accounted for ~62% of all phishing attacks targeting enterprise accounts.

Paragraph 2: This operation significantly degrades threat actors’ ability to compromise enterprise accounts at scale—especially in high-value sectors like healthcare, finance, and government. The disruption of Tycoon 2FA, one of the most prolific MFA bypass tools, marks a turning point in mitigating credential theft campaigns that have plagued organizations for over two years.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Actively Exploited in Espionage and Crypto-Theft Campaigns
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Google’s GTIG identified the Coruna iOS exploit kit—a sophisticated, nation-state-grade toolset comprising 23 zero-day and known vulnerabilities across five exploit chains targeting iOS 13.0 through 17.2.1. Coruna was originally used by commercial surveillance vendors, later repurposed by Russian APTs, and is now actively deployed in financially motivated attacks, including crypto wallet theft. The kit enables zero-click compromise of iPhones without requiring user interaction.

Paragraph 2: The proliferation of Coruna across threat actors—including state-sponsored and criminal groups—highlights a dangerous secondary market for high-end zero-day exploits. Its use in crypto-theft campaigns poses immediate risk to individuals and institutions holding digital assets, while its presence in espionage operations threatens national security and high-value corporate intelligence.

breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater APT Deploys New Dindoor Backdoor in U.S. Network Intrusions
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Iran-linked APT MuddyWater (aka SeedWorm, TA450, Static Kitten) has infiltrated U.S. networks—including banks, airports, nonprofits, and a software company’s Israeli branch—deploying a new backdoor named Dindoor. Activity began in February 2026 and continues actively. Broadcom’s Symantec Threat Hunter Team confirmed the compromise, noting the group’s use of custom implants and persistence mechanisms to maintain long-term access.

Paragraph 2: This campaign demonstrates the growing operational tempo of Iran’s cyber-intelligence operations against U.S. critical infrastructure and strategic sectors. The use of a previously unseen backdoor (Dindoor) suggests evolving tradecraft and long-term strategic intent, raising concerns about data exfiltration, surveillance, and potential preparation for disruptive operations.

breaking_news.title: Cisco Warns of Actively Exploited Catalyst SD-WAN Flaws CVE-2026-20128 and CVE-2026-20122
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Cisco confirmed active exploitation of two recently patched vulnerabilities in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager: CVE-2026-20128 (authentication bypass) and CVE-2026-20122 (arbitrary file overwrite). These flaws, patched February 25, allow attackers to gain root access, overwrite system files, and extract sensitive configuration data. Cisco updated its advisory on March 5 to warn customers that exploitation is underway in the wild.

Paragraph 2: Catalyst SD-WAN is widely deployed across enterprise and critical infrastructure networks globally. Active exploitation of these flaws poses severe risk of network-wide compromise, lateral movement, and persistent backdooring—especially given the central role of SD-WAN in modern network architecture and its privileged access to sensitive traffic and systems.

breaking_news.title: ClickFix Campaign Abuses Windows Terminal to Deliver Lumma Stealer
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Microsoft disclosed a widespread ClickFix campaign that bypasses traditional Run dialog detections by guiding victims to launch Windows Terminal (via Win+X → I) and paste malicious PowerShell commands. These commands deploy the Lumma Stealer, a credential-harvesting infostealer targeting browsers, password managers, and crypto wallets. The attack chain leverages fake CAPTCHA, troubleshooting, and verification lures to appear legitimate and evade user skepticism.

Paragraph 2: This evolution in ClickFix tactics demonstrates increasing sophistication in social engineering, specifically designed to bypass both technical controls and security awareness training. Lumma Stealer’s focus on credential and wallet theft poses significant financial and data risk to individuals and organizations, especially as Windows Terminal is a trusted system binary and bypasses many endpoint detection rules.

breaking_news.title: FBI Investigating Breach of Surveillance and Wiretap Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: The FBI confirmed it is investigating suspicious cyber activity on a network used to manage wiretaps and foreign intelligence surveillance warrants. A notification was sent to members of Congress, though the bureau declined to disclose further details. The incident is being treated as high-priority due to the sensitive nature of the systems involved and potential national security implications.

Paragraph 2: A breach of FBI surveillance infrastructure could expose classified intelligence methods, compromise ongoing investigations, and reveal sources and techniques used in counterintelligence operations. While attribution remains unconfirmed, the targeting of law enforcement surveillance systems is consistent with state-sponsored actors—particularly those with intelligence-gathering mandates.

breaking_news.title: Cognizant TriZetto Breach Exposes Health Data of 3.4 Million Patients
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: TriZetto Provider Solutions, a healthcare IT company serving health insurers and providers, suffered a data breach exposing the sensitive information of over 3.4 million individuals. The breach involved personally identifiable information (PII), protected health information (PHI), and financial data. Cognizant confirmed the incident and is working with law enforcement and forensic experts to determine the scope and vector.

Paragraph 2: This breach is among the largest healthcare data incidents in recent years and underscores the persistent threat to sensitive health data held by third-party vendors. Given TriZetto’s role in processing Medicare and Medicaid claims, the breach has broad implications for federal healthcare programs and could enable identity theft, insurance fraud, and targeted phishing against vulnerable populations.

breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked APT Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New Malware Families
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Zscaler ThreatLabz observed Iran-linked APT Dust Specter impersonating Iraq’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs in phishing emails to deliver four previously unseen malware families: SPLITDROP, TWINTASK, TWINTALK, and GHOSTFORM. The campaign, active since January 2026, uses multiple infection chains to establish persistent access and exfiltrate intelligence from high-value government targets.

Paragraph 2: The use of novel malware families indicates a deliberate effort to evade detection and maintain long-term access to Iraqi government systems. Given the geopolitical context—including regional tensions and U.S.-Israel operations in the Middle East—this activity likely supports strategic intelligence gathering and could be a precursor to more disruptive operations.

breaking_news.title: Over 100 GitHub Repositories Distributing BoryptGrab Stealer
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Researchers discovered over 100 GitHub repositories distributing the BoryptGrab infostealer, a modular malware family that steals browser credentials, cryptocurrency wallet data, system information, and user files. The campaign leverages SEO-optimized fake repositories and deceptive download pages to trick users into installing malicious installers. BoryptGrab deploys additional payloads including reverse SSH backdoors and proxy servers for command-and-control.

Paragraph 2: The scale and automation of this campaign—using GitHub as a distribution platform—demonstrates how open-source infrastructure is increasingly abused for malware delivery. With over 100 repositories active simultaneously, BoryptGrab poses a significant risk to individual users, developers, and enterprises, especially those with cryptocurrency holdings or sensitive development environments.
